reforms.description: The United States government increased its expenditure for basic research and development, aiming to enhance the country's innovation capacity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for basic research is expected to enhance innovation capacity, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While the reform is significant in increasing funding, it does not represent a structural change in the R&D funding system.
reforms.source_quote: an increase in government expenditure for basic research and development

reforms.description: The extension of the R&D tax credit for another year aims to incentivize private sector investment in research and development activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Incentivizing private sector investment in R&D is expected to stimulate innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The extension of the tax credit is significant for encouraging R&D investment but does not fundamentally change the tax system.
reforms.source_quote: an extension of the R&D tax credit for another year
